seo.title: 100 React (general) resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical React (general) resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource provides a technical roadmap for building and optimizing modern React applications using React 19, Vite, and the TanStack ecosystem. It focuses on production-ready patterns for state management, component architecture, and performance testing.

sections.heading: Modern State Management and Data Synchronization

items.title: Zustand Store Implementation
items.description: Create small, focused stores using the 'create' function to manage global UI state without the boilerplate of Redux or the re-render issues of Context.

items.title: React Query useQuery for Caching
items.description: Implement 'useQuery' with custom 'staleTime' and 'gcTime' to manage server-state fetching and automatic background revalidation.

items.title: Optimistic UI with React 19 useOptimistic
items.description: Use the 'useOptimistic' hook to provide immediate visual feedback during form submissions before the server responds.

items.title: Zod Schema Validation for Form State
items.description: Integrate Zod with React Hook Form to enforce strict type safety and runtime validation for complex user inputs.

items.title: Context API for Design Tokens
items.description: Limit Context usage to static or rarely changing data like theme modes (dark/light) or localization strings to avoid performance degradation.

items.title: Jotai for Atomic State
items.description: Apply Jotai atoms for granular state updates in performance-sensitive areas like canvas editors or complex dashboard widgets.

items.title: React Query Mutation Side Effects
items.description: Utilize the 'onSuccess' and 'onSettled' callbacks in 'useMutation' to trigger cache invalidations via 'queryClient.invalidateQueries'.

items.title: Zustand Middleware for Persistence
items.description: Apply the 'persist' middleware to automatically sync specific store slices to localStorage or sessionStorage.

items.title: Handling Server Actions
items.description: Implement React 19 Server Actions to handle form submissions directly, reducing the need for manual fetch calls and loading state management.

items.title: TanStack Query DevTools
items.description: Integrate the ReactQueryDevtools component to visualize query states, cache timing, and network fetch status during development.

items.title: Custom Hook Data Abstraction
items.description: Wrap React Query calls in custom hooks (e.g., useUser, useOrders) to decouple UI components from API endpoints.

items.title: React 19 useActionState Hook
items.description: Manage form state and server-side validation errors using the 'useActionState' hook for native-feeling form handling.

items.title: shadcn/ui Component Installation
items.description: Use the 'npx shadcn-ui@latest add' command to pull accessible, unstyled components directly into your local 'components/ui' folder.

items.title: Tailwind Merge for Dynamic Classes
items.description: Utilize 'twMerge' and 'clsx' utility functions to resolve Tailwind class conflicts in reusable component libraries.

items.title: Class Variance Authority (CVA)
items.description: Define component variants (size, color, intent) in a structured object to maintain type-safe CSS-in-JS patterns within Tailwind.

items.title: Radix UI Primitive Integration
items.description: Build custom UI components on top of Radix primitives to ensure keyboard navigation and screen reader support out of the box.

items.title: Compound Component Pattern
items.description: Structure components like Tabs or Select using a parent-child context relationship for flexible and readable JSX.

items.title: Storybook Interaction Testing
items.description: Write 'play' functions in Storybook to automate user interaction tests for UI components without launching a full browser environment.

items.title: Polymorphic Components with 'asChild'
items.description: Use the 'Slot' pattern from Radix UI to allow components to merge their props into a child element, improving HTML semantic flexibility.

items.title: Lucide React Icon Optimization
items.description: Import specific icons directly (e.g., import { Home } from 'lucide-react') to ensure tree-shaking keeps the bundle size minimal.

items.title: Framer Motion Layout Animations
items.description: Apply the 'layout' prop to Framer Motion components to automatically animate position changes when the DOM structure updates.

items.title: React Aria for Complex Inputs
items.description: Use Adobe’s React Aria hooks to implement high-accessibility behaviors for non-standard inputs like date range pickers.

items.title: Barrel Export Management
items.description: Avoid massive 'index.ts' barrel files in large projects to prevent Vite from over-parsing modules and slowing down HMR.

items.title: Container/Presenter Pattern Refactoring
items.description: Refactor complex components by separating data-fetching logic (containers) from pure UI rendering (presenters) for better testability.

items.title: React Compiler Integration
items.description: Enable the React Compiler in your build pipeline to automate 'useMemo' and 'useCallback' logic based on static analysis.

items.title: Vitest Unit Testing Setup
items.description: Configure Vitest with 'jsdom' and '@testing-library/react' for fast, Vite-native component unit tests.

items.title: Mock Service Worker (MSW) Setup
items.description: Intercept network requests at the service worker level to provide realistic API mocks for both testing and local development.

items.title: React Testing Library user-event
items.description: Replace 'fireEvent' with '@testing-library/user-event' to more accurately simulate browser events like typing and clicking.

items.title: useDeferredValue for Search Filtering
items.description: Implement 'useDeferredValue' to keep the UI responsive by deferring the re-rendering of large lists during rapid text input.

items.title: Rollup Plugin Visualizer
items.description: Add 'rollup-plugin-visualizer' to your Vite config to generate a treemap of dependency sizes and identify bloat.

items.title: React Profiler for Render Bottlenecks
items.description: Use the React DevTools Profiler tab to identify 'Commit' phase delays and unnecessary component re-renders.

items.title: Lazy Loading with React.lazy
items.description: Implement Code Splitting using 'React.lazy' and 'Suspense' for route-level components to reduce the initial JS payload.

items.title: Axe-core Accessibility Auditing
items.description: Integrate 'jest-axe' or 'vitest-axe' into your test suite to catch common WCAG violations automatically during CI/CD.

items.title: TanStack Router Type Safety
items.description: Migrate to TanStack Router to achieve 100% type safety for route parameters, search params, and navigation links.

items.title: Memoizing Expensive Computations
items.description: Apply 'useMemo' for heavy data transformations (e.g., sorting 1000+ items) to avoid recalculation on every render.

items.title: Eslint-plugin-react-refresh
items.description: Ensure 'eslint-plugin-react-refresh' is active to validate that your components are compatible with Fast Refresh during development.

pro_tips: Always use the 'key' prop to force-reset a component's internal state when switching between different data entities.
Avoid using 'useEffect' for data fetching; use a library like TanStack Query or React 19 Server Components to manage async state.
Utilize the 'AbortController' API inside 'useEffect' cleanup functions to cancel pending network requests when a component unmounts.
Structure your project by 'feature' folders (e.g., features/auth, features/billing) rather than generic folders like 'components' or 'hooks'.
Prefer 'useSyncExternalStore' over 'useEffect' when subscribing to browser APIs like window size or online status to ensure store consistency.
